;<span id="A-Factor">A-Factor</span> (absolute factor; AF)
:number associated with every [[Glossary:Element|element]] in a [[Glossary:Collection|collection]]. '''A-Factor''' determines how much [[Glossary:Interval|intervals]] increase in the learning process. The higher the '''A-Factor''', the faster the [[Glossary:Interval|intervals]] increase. You can see '''A-Factors''' in the [[Element data|'''Element data''' window]]. For [[Glossary:Item|items]], '''A-Factors''' reflect [[Glossary:Difficulty|item difficulty]]. For [[Glossary:Topic|topics]], '''A-Factors''' modulate topic priority (by affecting the frequency of exposition). The higher the '''A-Factor''' the easier the [[Glossary:Item|item]]. The most difficult [[Glossary:Item|items]] have '''A-Factors''' equal to 1.2. For [[Glossary:Item|items]], '''A-Factor''' is defined as the quotient of the second [[Glossary:Optimum_interval|optimum interval]] and the first optimum interval used in repetitions (read more: [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-15 SuperMemo Algorithm SM-15]). For [[Glossary:Topic|topics]] and [[Glossary:Task|tasks]], '''A-Factor''' is interpreted as the number by which the current [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] should be multiplied to get the value of the next [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] (as it was the case with [[Glossary:E-Factor|E-Factors]] in early versions of '''SuperMemo'''). '''Important!''' Algorithm SM-17 (and later), do not use A-factors. Their meaning is unchanged, but for your information only (and for Algorithm SM-15 that runs in parallel in the background)
